subject: IceCube Alert 250207.09: Global MASTER-Net observations report
submitter: Vladimir Lipunov at Moscow State U/Krylov Obs <lipunov@xray.sai.msu.ru>
body:
V.Lipunov, E.Gorbovskoy, N.Tiurina,  P.Balanutsa, , D.Vlasenko, I.Panchenko,
A.Kuznetsov,  G.Antipov,  A.Sankovich, A.Sosnovskij, Yu.Tselik, M.Gulyaev, Ya.Kechin,

C.Francile,  F. Podesta, R.Podesta, E. Gonzalez  (Observatorio Astronomico Felix Aguilar (OAFA),

A. Gabovich, V.Yurkov (Blagoveschensk Educational StateUniversity)

R.Rebolo (The Instituto de Astrofisica de Canarias),
L.Carrasco, J.R.Valdes, V.Chavushyan, V.M.Patino Alvarez, J.Martinez,
A.R.Corella, L.H.Rodriguez (INAOE, Guillermo Haro Astrophysics Observatory) 

MASTER-SAAO robotic telescope  (Global MASTER-Net: http://observ.pereplet.ru, Lipunov et al., 2010, Advances in Astronomy, vol. 2010, 30L)  located in South Africa (South African Astronomical Observatory) started inspect of the IceCube Alert 250207.09 (trigger No 78196104,08h 41m 03.60s , +17d 02m 34.8s, R=4.44) errorbox  14 sec after notice time and 68 sec after trigger time at 2025-02-07 02:09:04 UT, with upper limit up to  19.0 mag. The observations began at zenith distance = 75 deg. The sun  altitude  is -23.3 deg. 

MASTER-Tunka robotic telescope  located in Russia (Applied Physics Institute, Irkutsk State University) started inspect of the IceCube Alert 250207.09 errorbox  42255 sec after notice time and 42309 sec after trigger time at 2025-02-07 13:53:05 UT, with upper limit up to  19.2 mag. The observations began at zenith distance = 45 deg. The sun  altitude  is -35.4 deg. 

The galactic latitude b = 32 deg., longitude l = 209 deg.


Real time updated cover map and OT discovered available here: 
https://master.sai.msu.ru/site/master2/observ.php?id=2770114

We obtain a following upper limits.
